Trustee, Darnis A. Banks Petition 1) For Approval Of Distribution; 2) Terminating Trust; And 3) Discharge Of Trustee
Off Calendar Aug-31-2020 Continued To Oct-19-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Konstantine Demiris appeared by zoom for Darnis Banks (Trustee).

The Court noted that there has been no response to the Probate Examiner's notes. Mr. Demiris stated that he has worked with Counsel for the other two remainder beneficiaries on getting the paper work for waivers of the accounting, that he has only received one waiver, that he has not received the other one and that it is his understanding that it is on its way. Mr. Demiris also addressed the issue raised in the Probate Examiner's notes regarding the administration of trusts is intended to proceed expeditiously and free of judicial intervention stating that he is here because they had a settlement agreement that requires the Court's review of the matter. The Trustee's Petition for Approval of Distribution, etc. is continued to October 19, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Cabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-11-2018 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Heather Maslowski for Darnis Banks (Trustee).
Collin Kelley and Richard Romano for Darryl Simmons and Barbara Banks (Beneficiaries).

The Court noted that the parties have now seemed to have reached an agreement. Ms. Maslowski stated that they have worked together to seek approval of the accounting that was provided. Mr. Collin stated that the reason they are here is because there is a blocked account and they need an order to release the funds. Ms. Maslowksi stated that the amount in the account increased by $20.00 and that they are seeking to request that the amount to Darnis Banks be increased by $20.00. The Court stated that everything is off calendar, except that the Court will issue an order for the blocked account once it is received and reviewed. The Petition to Approve First and Final Account and Report, etc. is off calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Anasse. Not reported.

Petition For Withdrawal Of Funds From Blocked Account
Off Calendar Dec-21-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Konstantine A. Demiris for petitioner Darnis Banks; Collin Kelly and Richard Romano for Barbara Banks and Harold Simmons>

The Court inquired if there was any objection to the petition. All counsel responded that they had no objection and that they were not aware of any objection to the petition. Mr. Demiris reported that there were still a few rremaining liabilities. He stated that if the proposed withdrawal were approved, there will still be over $100,000 remaining in the blocked account, which should be adequate to cover the remaining liabilities.

The Court granted the petition (subject to review of the order). Counsel are to be notified by telephone when the order has been entered.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not reported)

Petition For Order Confirming That Real And Personal Property Is Trust Property
Off Calendar Jan-22-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Christopher Moore for Darnis Banks via court call. Colin Kelley for (respondents) Barbara Banks & Darrell Simmons.

Mr. Kelley confirmed that his clients objections have been withdrawn in connection with the settlement reached. Mr. Moore stated that he agrees that the prayer does not include extra items of property, that the petition itself includes allegations that are broader, that he believes everyone has received adequate notice of what is going on and that he was okay with the court conforming the order. The petition for order confirming that real and personal property is trust property was taken under submission (subject to the court striking/conforming the proposed order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/ Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Benton. (Not Reported)

Petition For Order Confirming That Real And Personal Property Is Trust Property

Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement By Respondent Darrell Simmons
Off Calendar Dec-19-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Romano and Collin Kelley for Darrell Simmons and Barbara Banks. Konstantine Demiris appeared by telephone for Darnis Banks.

The Court raised the issue of no notice being given Natalie Padia, the suggestion that Hattie Faye Butler is incapacitated but signed a non opposition and the propriety of having bargained down potential distribution rights of those persons that were not party to the settlement agreement. Mr. Kelley stated that Natalie Padia died in April of 2008, that he has a declaration from the daughter but no death certificate, that there was miscommunication between Counsel as to Ms. Butler's incapacity and that she is in a residential care facility. Mr. Demiris spoke of Ms. Butler's medical condition, that she had the ability to sign the document and stated that they received correspondence the attorney in Louisiana. Mr. Kelley also stated that the settlement amounts are reasonable and that there was no guarantee the parties would have received anything had the will not been admitted. The Petition for Approval of Settlement Agreement was taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)
